cuobjdump.name: cuobjdump
cuobjdump.version: cuobjdump: NVIDIA (R) fat binary listing tool

ncu.name: ncu
ncu.version: NVIDIA (R) Nsight Compute Command Line Profiler

interpretation.nvidia_backend: device identity is available; resource limits need CUDA API helper in the next cutline

assumptions: nvidia-smi identity metadata is treated as direct runtime metadata
published_facts are curated trust-and-verify anchors, not runtime measurements
normalized_measurement.name: cuda_device_identity

simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: identity metadata is recorded for traceability and is not a simulator structural parameter

cuobjdump.name: cuobjdump
cuobjdump.version: cuobjdump: NVIDIA (R) fat binary listing tool

ncu.name: ncu
ncu.version: NVIDIA (R) Nsight Compute Command Line Profiler

interpretation.nvidia_backend: block/register/shared-memory cross-product for downstream occupancy fits

assumptions: occupancy sweep is a planning artifact; resident-block fitting requires CUDA Occupancy API
normalized_measurement.name: occupancy_sweep_plan

assumptions: occupancy sweep is a planning artifact; resident-block fitting requires CUDA Occupancy API
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: planning artifact; not a structural simulator parameter

interpretation.nvidia_backend: peak resident CTAs per SM under the configured launch shape

assumptions: concurrency derived from sweep-line over per-SM (start,end) cycle pairs
kernel uses %smid plus a busy-spin to keep blocks resident long enough to overlap
normalized_measurement.name: resident_blocks_per_sm

assumptions: concurrency derived from sweep-line over per-SM (start,end) cycle pairs
kernel uses %smid plus a busy-spin to keep blocks resident long enough to overlap
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: observed peak block residency under busy-spin → simulator max_resident_ctas_per_sm

interpretation.nvidia_backend: cycles between issue and writeback for a dependent FMA

assumptions: FP32 FMA dependent chain timed via clock64 inside a single warp
median across N launches is reported to suppress one-shot kernel-launch jitter
normalized_measurement.name: fp32_fma_dependent_latency

assumptions: FP32 FMA dependent chain timed via clock64 inside a single warp
median across N launches is reported to suppress one-shot kernel-launch jitter
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: dependent FMA cycles-per-op → simulator FP32 FMA latency depth

interpretation.nvidia_backend: effective FMA cycles-per-op once ILP saturates the FP32 pipe

assumptions: 4 independent FMA chains per thread to expose ILP
throughput is per-thread cycles-per-op; per-SM is approximate (assumes resident across all SMs)
normalized_measurement.name: fp32_fma_throughput

assumptions: 4 independent FMA chains per thread to expose ILP
throughput is per-thread cycles-per-op; per-SM is approximate (assumes resident across all SMs)
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: independent FMA cycles-per-op → simulator FP32 FMA throughput

interpretation.nvidia_backend: LDS dependent-load latency in cycles

assumptions: single-thread pointer chase over a 1024-entry shared-memory ring
median cycles-per-load is reported across N kernel launches
normalized_measurement.name: shared_memory_load_latency

assumptions: single-thread pointer chase over a 1024-entry shared-memory ring
median cycles-per-load is reported across N kernel launches
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: dependent shared-memory chase cycles-per-load → simulator shared-mem latency

interpretation.nvidia_backend: shared-memory bank count inferred from cycles-per-access vs stride curve

assumptions: single warp probes shared memory with stride sweep covering conflict-factors 1..32
conflict factor reported as gcd(stride, 32) which holds for shipping NVIDIA archs
normalized_measurement.name: shared_memory_bank_count

assumptions: single warp probes shared memory with stride sweep covering conflict-factors 1..32
conflict factor reported as gcd(stride, 32) which holds for shipping NVIDIA archs
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: bank-stride sweep peak conflict factor → simulator shared-memory bank count

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged shared-memory characterization derived from pointer-chase and bank-stride probes

assumptions: consumes the cycles-per-load median from pointer_chase and the stride sweep from bank_stride
bank_serialization_factor is full_conflict / no_conflict cycles-per-access
normalized_measurement.name: shared_memory_summary

assumptions: consumes the cycles-per-load median from pointer_chase and the stride sweep from bank_stride
bank_serialization_factor is full_conflict / no_conflict cycles-per-access
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe summary suitable for simulator shared-memory model parameters

interpretation.nvidia_backend: dependent-load latency for an L1-resident working set in cycles

assumptions: single-thread dependent pointer chase over a randomized ring sized to fit L1
a DRAM-resident ring is timed as a control; L1-hit regime requires small << large
median cycles-per-load reported across N launches
normalized_measurement.name: l1_hit_load_latency

assumptions: single-thread dependent pointer chase over a randomized ring sized to fit L1
a DRAM-resident ring is timed as a control; L1-hit regime requires small << large
median cycles-per-load reported across N launches
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: dependent L1-hit chase cycles-per-load → simulator L1 hit latency

backend_interpretation.downgrade_reason: no clear capacity knee detected in the sweep

interpretation.nvidia_backend: effective L1 capacity bounded by the first latency knee in the working-set sweep

assumptions: dependent pointer-chase latency swept across geometric working-set sizes
first >40% latency jump marks the effective L1 capacity knee
capacity is reported as a bounded range, not an exact scalar
normalized_measurement.name: l1_effective_capacity

assumptions: dependent pointer-chase latency swept across geometric working-set sizes
first >40% latency jump marks the effective L1 capacity knee
capacity is reported as a bounded range, not an exact scalar
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: working-set latency knee → simulator L1 capacity range

interpretation.nvidia_backend: effective L1 associativity bounded by the conflict-set latency knee

assumptions: ring of same-set lines grown one way at a time at a fixed power-of-two stride
latency knee marks where the conflict set exceeds the effective associativity
associativity is bounded: indexing/replacement/hashing can mimic the same curve
normalized_measurement.name: l1_effective_associativity

assumptions: ring of same-set lines grown one way at a time at a fixed power-of-two stride
latency knee marks where the conflict set exceeds the effective associativity
associativity is bounded: indexing/replacement/hashing can mimic the same curve
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: conflict-set latency knee → simulator L1 associativity (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged L1 path characterization from latency, capacity, and conflict probes

assumptions: merges L1 hit latency, capacity knee, and associativity knee
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
normalized_measurement.name: l1_cache_summary

assumptions: merges L1 hit latency, capacity knee, and associativity knee
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe L1 summary for simulator L1 cache model parameters

interpretation.nvidia_backend: ready-warp count at which issue throughput saturates on one SM

assumptions: one CTA on one SM runs N independent dependent-FMA warps (no memory)
saturation warp count = smallest warp count reaching 95% of peak ops/cycle
scheduler policy name is behavioral; only issue scaling is reported
normalized_measurement.name: issue_saturation_warps

assumptions: one CTA on one SM runs N independent dependent-FMA warps (no memory)
saturation warp count = smallest warp count reaching 95% of peak ops/cycle
scheduler policy name is behavioral; only issue scaling is reported
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: issue-scaling saturation knee → simulator scheduler issue capacity (conditional)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: FP32/INT pipe overlap classified from mixed vs single-pipe throughput

assumptions: independent FP32 (FMA) and INT (MAD) streams run alone and interleaved
overlap_ratio = mixed / max(fp32, int); higher means more pipe overlap
mixed-issue capability is a behavioral class, not a named policy
normalized_measurement.name: mixed_issue_overlap

assumptions: independent FP32 (FMA) and INT (MAD) streams run alone and interleaved
overlap_ratio = mixed / max(fp32, int); higher means more pipe overlap
mixed-issue capability is a behavioral class, not a named policy
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: mixed/single-pipe overlap ratio → simulator dual-issue behavioral class

interpretation.nvidia_backend: scheduler issue scaling and pipe-overlap behavior on one SM

assumptions: combines ready-warp issue saturation with mixed-issue overlap class
scheduler policy reported as a behavioral class with a conditional issue-capacity value
normalized_measurement.name: scheduler_summary

assumptions: combines ready-warp issue saturation with mixed-issue overlap class
scheduler policy reported as a behavioral class with a conditional issue-capacity value
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe scheduler summary for simulator scheduler model (behavioral)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: operand-delivery throughput plateau across register-pressure widths

assumptions: operand-width sweep of independent FMA accumulators (register pressure proxy)
SASS confirms distinct FFMA register operands so the sweep is register-controlled
plateau width marks where added ILP stops improving cycles-per-op
normalized_measurement.name: operand_delivery_plateau

assumptions: operand-width sweep of independent FMA accumulators (register pressure proxy)
SASS confirms distinct FFMA register operands so the sweep is register-controlled
plateau width marks where added ILP stops improving cycles-per-op
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: operand-width plateau → simulator register-bank pressure (candidate, multi-fit)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: extra per-op cost of tight RAW dependence attributable to operand delivery

assumptions: same-register (RAW distance 1) vs rotating-register (relaxed RAW) chains of equal length
differential cycles-per-op isolates operand-delivery cost from absolute arithmetic latency
operand-collector parameters stay conditional: scoreboard/bank effects are entangled
normalized_measurement.name: operand_delivery_differential_latency

assumptions: same-register (RAW distance 1) vs rotating-register (relaxed RAW) chains of equal length
differential cycles-per-op isolates operand-delivery cost from absolute arithmetic latency
operand-collector parameters stay conditional: scoreboard/bank effects are entangled
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: RAW-distance differential cycles → simulator operand-delivery latency (conditional)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged register-bank pressure and operand-delivery differential latency

assumptions: keeps register-bank evidence (plateau) separate from operand-delivery latency (differential)
bank count remains a candidate; differential latency remains conditional
normalized_measurement.name: register_file_summary

assumptions: keeps register-bank evidence (plateau) separate from operand-delivery latency (differential)
bank count remains a candidate; differential latency remains conditional
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe register-file summary for simulator operand-delivery model (candidate)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: cycles per __syncthreads() barrier for the measured CTA shape

assumptions: one CTA runs a long __syncthreads() loop with minimal inter-barrier work
cycles-per-barrier reported for the smallest block; scaling curve retained
barrier cost is occupancy-coupled; reported per the launch class measured
normalized_measurement.name: cta_barrier_latency

assumptions: one CTA runs a long __syncthreads() loop with minimal inter-barrier work
cycles-per-barrier reported for the smallest block; scaling curve retained
barrier cost is occupancy-coupled; reported per the launch class measured
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cycles-per-barrier for a named CTA shape → simulator barrier latency (conditional)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: sustained DRAM/HBM bandwidth per traffic class from streaming kernels

assumptions: grid-stride read/write/copy over a working set far larger than cache
best-of-N CUDA-event timing; bandwidth is bounded by clock variation
copy moves 2x bytes (read+write); reported as achieved sustained GB/s
normalized_measurement.name: dram_sustained_bandwidth

assumptions: grid-stride read/write/copy over a working set far larger than cache
best-of-N CUDA-event timing; bandwidth is bounded by clock variation
copy moves 2x bytes (read+write); reported as achieved sustained GB/s
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: achieved sustained bandwidth per traffic class → simulator DRAM bandwidth (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: dependent-load latency for an L2-resident working set in cycles

assumptions: single-thread dependent pointer chase over a randomized ring sized to exceed L1 but fit L2
a DRAM-resident ring is timed as a control; L2-hit regime requires l2 << dram
median cycles-per-load reported across N launches
normalized_measurement.name: l2_hit_load_latency

assumptions: single-thread dependent pointer chase over a randomized ring sized to exceed L1 but fit L2
a DRAM-resident ring is timed as a control; L2-hit regime requires l2 << dram
median cycles-per-load reported across N launches
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: dependent L2-resident chase cycles-per-load -> simulator L2 hit latency (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: in-flight independent loads at which memory throughput saturates

assumptions: each thread issues a swept number of independent global loads before consuming them
a single wave of blocks is launched so throughput is bound by outstanding-request capacity
saturation knee = smallest in-flight count reaching >=95% of peak bytes/cycle
normalized_measurement.name: effective_outstanding_requests

assumptions: each thread issues a swept number of independent global loads before consuming them
a single wave of blocks is launched so throughput is bound by outstanding-request capacity
saturation knee = smallest in-flight count reaching >=95% of peak bytes/cycle
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: outstanding-load saturation knee -> simulator load/store queue capacity (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: global-load sectors per request from controlled lane address patterns

assumptions: one warp issues many global loads under named lane address patterns
NCU sectors/request is the primary coalescing signal; timing only confirms LDG activity
max counter value across profiled lane patterns characterizes the worst-case coalescing
normalized_measurement.name: coalescing_sectors_per_request

assumptions: one warp issues many global loads under named lane address patterns
NCU sectors/request is the primary coalescing signal; timing only confirms LDG activity
max counter value across profiled lane patterns characterizes the worst-case coalescing
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: NCU sectors/request under lane patterns -> simulator memory coalescing rule

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged memory-pipeline characterization from coalescing and outstanding-request probes

assumptions: merges lane-pattern coalescing (sectors/request) with the outstanding-request knee
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
normalized_measurement.name: memory_pipeline_summary

assumptions: merges lane-pattern coalescing (sectors/request) with the outstanding-request knee
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe memory-pipeline summary for simulator coalescing + load/store-queue parameters

interpretation.nvidia_backend: DRAM partition-camping sensitivity from base-offset bandwidth sweep

assumptions: grid-stride read from several base offsets relative to the partition interleave
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per offset; bandwidth varies with clock/partition balance
max/min bandwidth ratio < 1.15 classifies as balanced else camping_sensitive
normalized_measurement.name: partition_camping_class

assumptions: grid-stride read from several base offsets relative to the partition interleave
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per offset; bandwidth varies with clock/partition balance
max/min bandwidth ratio < 1.15 classifies as balanced else camping_sensitive
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: base-offset bandwidth variation -> simulator memory-partition camping class

interpretation.nvidia_backend: DRAM row-locality sensitivity from a stride bandwidth sweep

assumptions: grid-stride read with several element strides to vary DRAM row-buffer locality
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per stride; bandwidth bounded by clock variation
row_locality_sensitivity = best_gbps / worst_gbps across strides (bounded)
normalized_measurement.name: row_locality_sensitivity

assumptions: grid-stride read with several element strides to vary DRAM row-buffer locality
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per stride; bandwidth bounded by clock variation
row_locality_sensitivity = best_gbps / worst_gbps across strides (bounded)
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: stride bandwidth spread -> simulator DRAM row-buffer policy class (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged global-memory characterization from streaming, partition, and row-locality probes

assumptions: merges streaming peak bandwidth, partition-camping class, and row-locality sensitivity
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
normalized_measurement.name: global_memory_summary

assumptions: merges streaming peak bandwidth, partition-camping class, and row-locality sensitivity
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe global-memory summary for simulator DRAM bandwidth + partition + row-policy parameters

interpretation.nvidia_backend: dependent FP16 16x16x16 MMA latency in cycles

assumptions: dependent wmma::mma_sync chain (FP16 m16n16k16) timed via clock64 in one warp
median across launches
normalized_measurement.name: tensor_mma_latency

assumptions: dependent wmma::mma_sync chain (FP16 m16n16k16) timed via clock64 in one warp
median across launches
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: dependent MMA cycles-per-op -> simulator tensor-core pipeline latency

interpretation.nvidia_backend: independent FP16 16x16x16 MMA throughput in MMA-ops per cycle per warp

assumptions: independent wmma::mma_sync accumulators (FP16 m16n16k16) expose ILP to saturate the tensor pipe
median across launches; throughput reported per warp
normalized_measurement.name: tensor_mma_throughput

assumptions: independent wmma::mma_sync accumulators (FP16 m16n16k16) expose ILP to saturate the tensor pipe
median across launches; throughput reported per warp
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: independent MMA throughput -> simulator tensor-core initiation interval

interpretation.nvidia_backend: net cycles per __threadfence() after subtracting empty-loop overhead

assumptions: device-scope __threadfence() loop timed via clock64 in one CTA
empty-loop baseline subtracted to remove loop/branch overhead from the per-fence cost
net per-fence cost reflects fence scope as measured; median across launches
normalized_measurement.name: memory_fence_latency

assumptions: device-scope __threadfence() loop timed via clock64 in one CTA
empty-loop baseline subtracted to remove loop/branch overhead from the per-fence cost
net per-fence cost reflects fence scope as measured; median across launches
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: net per-fence cycles -> simulator memory fence latency (conditional)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: cycles per cp.async-staged tile (issue->wait->use)

assumptions: one CTA stages tiles global->shared via cp.async (__pipeline_memcpy_async)
cycles-per-tile brackets issue->commit->wait->use with clock64()
completion latency is conditional on cp.async being emitted (LDGSTS)
normalized_measurement.name: async_copy_tile_latency

assumptions: one CTA stages tiles global->shared via cp.async (__pipeline_memcpy_async)
cycles-per-tile brackets issue->commit->wait->use with clock64()
completion latency is conditional on cp.async being emitted (LDGSTS)
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: async-copy issue->wait->use cycles -> simulator async-copy completion latency (conditional)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: peak achieved cp.async global->shared bandwidth across tile sizes

assumptions: one CTA bulk-stages global->shared via cp.async over a tile-size sweep
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per size; throughput is bounded by clock variation
peak GB/s across the sweep is reported as achieved async-copy bandwidth
normalized_measurement.name: async_copy_throughput

assumptions: one CTA bulk-stages global->shared via cp.async over a tile-size sweep
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per size; throughput is bounded by clock variation
peak GB/s across the sweep is reported as achieved async-copy bandwidth
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: peak async-copy bandwidth across tile sizes -> simulator async-copy transfer throughput (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged async-copy characterization from latency and throughput probes

assumptions: merges async-copy tile latency and peak async-copy throughput
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
normalized_measurement.name: async_copy_summary

assumptions: merges async-copy tile latency and peak async-copy throughput
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe async-copy summary for simulator async-copy latency + throughput parameters

interpretation.nvidia_backend: peak aggregate injection bandwidth vs offered load (blocks per SM)

assumptions: multi-SM grid-stride stream over a working set far larger than cache
offered load swept via blocks-per-SM = {1,2,4,8}; best-of-N CUDA-event timing
peak aggregate GB/s across offered loads is the injection-saturation bandwidth
normalized_measurement.name: injection_saturation_gbps

assumptions: multi-SM grid-stride stream over a working set far larger than cache
offered load swept via blocks-per-SM = {1,2,4,8}; best-of-N CUDA-event timing
peak aggregate GB/s across offered loads is the injection-saturation bandwidth
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: peak aggregate injection bandwidth vs offered load -> simulator interconnect injection bandwidth (bounded)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: partition/slice periodicity from base-stride bandwidth variation

assumptions: grid-stride reads with a per-step base displacement swept across power-of-two strides
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per stride; bandwidth varies with partition interleave
max/min bandwidth ratio < 1.2 classifies as uniform else periodic_camping
normalized_measurement.name: address_mapping_class

assumptions: grid-stride reads with a per-step base displacement swept across power-of-two strides
best-of-N CUDA-event timing per stride; bandwidth varies with partition interleave
max/min bandwidth ratio < 1.2 classifies as uniform else periodic_camping
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: base-stride bandwidth variation -> simulator address-partition mapping class (candidate/behavioral)

interpretation.nvidia_backend: merged interconnect characterization from injection-rate and address-mapping probes

assumptions: merges injection-saturation bandwidth and address-mapping behavioral class
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
normalized_measurement.name: interconnect_summary

assumptions: merges injection-saturation bandwidth and address-mapping behavioral class
merged fit status is the weakest of the contributing probe fits
simulator_estimate.mapping_contract: cross-probe interconnect summary for simulator injection bandwidth + address-mapping parameters
